name: Fort Sill
tagline: The Army's Fires Center of Excellence, home of the Field Artillery and Air Defense Artillery, in southwest Oklahoma.
_note: Army post (Lawton OK, ~85 mi SW of OKC). Facts verified from official sources (sill-www.army.mil, reynolds.tricare.mil, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). BAH MHA OK237 (Fort Sill/Lawton) via the bah_rates single source of truth. Housing partner not named pending confirmation (start with the housing office). Oklahoma is TRICARE West (moved from East in 2024). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), representative off-base ZIP 73505 (Lawton).

data.Home of: The Field Artillery and Air Defense Artillery branches
data.Also: Basic Combat Training and artillery AIT

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Sill is the Army's Fires Center of Excellence and the home of the Field Artillery and Air Defense Artillery branches, the premier fires training installation in the Army. It covers nearly 94,000 acres in southwest Oklahoma north of Lawton.
Along with branch training, Fort Sill runs Basic Combat Training, so much of the population is here to train, instruct, or support.

data.subtitle: Fort Sill is among the most affordable assignments in the Army, so BAH goes a long way. Lawton is the close-in, convenient choice, and the post's training mission keeps the community busy.
intro: As a fires and training hub, Fort Sill cycles a lot of students and instructors, with a steady, friendly community around the post.
Lawton, just south of the gates, is inexpensive and convenient, with most families either living there or on post. Oklahoma City is about 85 miles northeast for a bigger-city day out.

recommendations.advice: Lawton sits right outside the post with low rents and home prices and a short commute.
On-post housing is available; start with the housing office, as on-base can be a strong value here.
Costs are low, so BAH tends to cover a comfortable home with room to spare.
Oklahoma City, about 85 miles northeast, offers more shopping, dining, and an airport for a day or weekend.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Sill site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Sill has privatized on-post family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA OK237 (Fort Sill/Lawton)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools, primarily Lawton Public Schools, with some smaller districts serving outlying areas.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and specialty care plus an urgent-care walk-in clinic; inpatient care referred to Lawton-area hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or you're here for a course.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a neighborhood.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable Lawton market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Lawton is very affordable, so BAH stretches well, though resale can be slower in a smaller market. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Lawton neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Lawton Public Schools early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Reynolds Army Health Clinic or network providers.

data.a: It's the Army's Fires Center of Excellence, home of the Field Artillery and Air Defense Artillery branches, and it also runs Basic Combat Training.
Lawton, right outside the gates, is the close-in and affordable choice; on-post housing is also available.
Very. Fort Sill is among the most affordable Army assignments, so BAH stretches a long way.
Fort Sill is MHA OK237 (Fort Sill/Lawton). Use the calculator above for your grade.
